name: Kash Varnishkes (Rose Levy Beranbaum)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Plus 1 tbsp Goose fat or butter
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak porcini mushrooms in a small amount of warm water (or enough to make up the 3/4 cup chicken broth). Let them soak until soft.
2. Cut the soaked mushrooms into small pieces and set aside.
3. In a bowl, stir together kasha and egg. Set aside for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Melt 1/4 cup goose fat in a large skillet (preferably broiler-proof) with a tight-fitting lid. Add onions along with sugar and sauté, stirring often, until well-browned.
5. Add mushrooms and garlic to the skillet. Cover and cook until the mushrooms release their liquid. Continue cooking uncovered until the liquid evaporates.
6. Add the kasha mixture, salt, pepper, and oregano. Cook, stirring constantly for 2 or 3 minutes.
7. Stir in the broth and porcini mushrooms. Cover tightly and simmer for 20 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed.
8. Meanwhile, cook bowtie noodles according to package directions. Drain and stir in the remaining 1 tbsp goose fat. Mix the cooked bowties into the kasha mixture.
9. Makes 4 to 6 servings.
